title: DB schema not applied on server start, only on init
description: db.rs connect() opens the SQLite database with create_if_missing(true) but does NOT run the schema SQL. The schema is only applied in init_data_dir(). If the database file exists but was created by an older version (missing tables/columns), or if someone runs 'broadside serve' before 'broadside init', the server will start but crash on the first query that touches a missing table. The connect-then-serve path in server.rs:serve() calls db::connect() directly without ensuring the schema exists. Fix: run the schema DDL (which uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) on every connect, or at least on server startup.
close_reason: db::connect now runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema on every connect
